name: Humidification Therapy
description: Humidification of the breathing circuit: the humidification procedure and the humidifier device (incl. heat-and-moisture exchanger).

en.name: Humidification Therapy
en.shortDescription: Humidification of the breathing circuit: the humidification procedure and the humidifier device (incl. heat-and-moisture exchanger).
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Humidification therapy adds warmth and moisture to the gas a patient breathes. When a patient is intubated or on high-flow oxygen, the upper airway — which normally humidifies inhaled air — is bypassed, so the breathing circuit must humidify the gas to keep secretions thin and protect the airway lining. This concept set captures two kinds of records: the **procedure** (the patient received humidification) and the **device** used (a humidifier or a heat-and-moisture exchanger). It is recorded mainly in intensive care and anaesthesia for ventilated or high-flow-oxygen patients. These are event/device records, not numeric measurements, so there is no unit.

## Included Concepts

The set is built from two anchors plus the passive heat-and-moisture exchanger device, grouped here by what they represent.

### Humidification procedure

Anchored on *Humidification therapy* with its descendants (the traditional menthol/steam/fog/mist inhalation therapies are excluded — see Excluded Concepts).

- *4055258 / 243130008 — Humidification therapy (SNOMED)*: the general procedure; the default for "patient received humidification".
- *4116405 / 286814009 — Inspired gas humidification (SNOMED)*: humidification of the inspired gas in a breathing circuit.
- *4042359 / 229302002 — Ultrasonic humidification (SNOMED)*: humidification produced by an ultrasonic nebuliser.

### Humidifier device

Anchored on *Respiratory humidifier* with its descendants, covering the humidifier types used in breathing circuits, plus the passive heat-and-moisture exchanger.

- *45768203 / 706180003 — Respiratory humidifier (SNOMED)*: the general respiratory humidifier device.
- *45764021 / 700593005 — Heated respiratory humidifier (SNOMED)*: active heated humidifier.
- *45773241 / 700705005 — Non-heated respiratory humidifier (SNOMED)*: unheated humidifier.
- *45764272 / 700910000 — Ultrasonic respiratory humidifier (SNOMED)*: ultrasonic humidifier device.
- *45764051 / 700630004 — High-flow heated respiratory humidifier (SNOMED)*: heated humidifier for high-flow circuits.
- *1246607 / 48621000087108 — High-flow non-heated respiratory humidifier (SNOMED)*: unheated humidifier for high-flow circuits.
- *4178391 / 297117007 — Heat and moisture exchanger (SNOMED)*: a passive device ("artificial nose") placed in the circuit that traps the patient's exhaled heat and moisture and returns them on the next breath; an alternative to an active humidifier.

## Excluded Concepts

The traditional inhalation-therapy branch under *Humidification therapy* is excluded, as it covers home/ENT remedies rather than ventilation-circuit humidification:

- *4039397 / 229303007 — Mist inhalation therapy (SNOMED)* and its descendants *4039398 / 229304001 — Steam inhalations*, *4039921 / 229305000 — Menthol inhalations*, and *4215616 / 80677001 — Fog inhalation therapy* (all SNOMED).

## Mapping Notes

- **This set mixes procedures and devices.** Map a humidification *event* to the procedure concepts and a humidifier *device* to the device concepts. Do not mix the two kinds.
- **Default for an unspecified humidification procedure**: *4055258 / 243130008 — Humidification therapy*. Use *4116405 / 286814009 — Inspired gas humidification* when the source specifically describes humidifying the inspired gas of a breathing circuit.
- **Default for an unspecified humidifier device**: *45768203 / 706180003 — Respiratory humidifier*. Map to a specific type (heated, non-heated, ultrasonic, high-flow) only when the source states it.
- **Active humidifier vs HME**: map a passive heat-and-moisture exchanger ("artificial nose") to *4178391 / 297117007 — Heat and moisture exchanger*, not to a humidifier device — they are different pieces of equipment.
- **Humidifier temperature settings** (chamber temperature, target temperature, heated-wire differential) are numeric measurements and are intentionally not part of this set, which is limited to procedures and devices.
- **Traditional inhalation therapies** (steam, menthol, fog, mist) are not part of this set and should not be mapped here.

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Restructure on two anchors, procedures + devices only (homogeneous set): Humidification therapy (4055258, descendants, excluding the traditional mist/steam/menthol/fog inhalation branch 4039397) and Respiratory humidifier device (45768203, descendants) + Heat and moisture exchanger (4178391). Humidifier temperature measurements removed (kept out to keep the set homogeneous). Resolves to 10. Added clinical description.
